column1: # fapolicyd probe transcript - dataset: e07 (issue #478 grounding recon)
# Format: TSV, 5 columns, no header row below this comment block:
# id       = <attr>_<shape> ; shape in {int, str, mixed, signed_negfirst}
# verdict  = "accept" (daemon loaded the rule) or "reject" (parse error)
# loaded_n = the "Loaded N rules" count from fapolicyd stdout (0 if rejected)
#            file: a `%t=<values>` set definition followed by one rule
#            assigning `%t` to the attribute under test, on its correct side
#            (subject attrs use `attr=%t : all`; object attrs - path, mode -
#            use `exe=/usr/bin/probe : attr=%t`, NOT bare `all` on the
#            subject side - bare `all` on the subject side is itself a
#            SYNTAX error on the real daemon, independent of any macro; see
# Ground-truth parse gate: same ACCEPT/REJECT rule as the pattern dataset.
# Lines starting with "#" are comments; a derive tool should skip them.
